patterns.label: Azure DevOps Eval Work-Item Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36 confirms 30 ADO email notifications again (freq=30, 45min) plus 4 DevOps-Alert-to-Sync-to-Doc workflow triggers (+34 occurrences). Cumulative 94 across 14 cycles. Pattern fully INSTITUTIONAL — consistent 30/week volume, machine-parseable subject line patterns, deterministic routing logic. Sub-classifiers confirmed: IcM, Coverage, Evals, Tasks, Epics, plus Alert-to-Sync pipeline. Automation ceiling 90-95%. No drift — pattern plateaued and mature. Score held at 93 per 14-cycle evidence base.

patterns.label: Inbound Eval Strategy and Guidance Q&A (Expert Bottleneck)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 10 new Eval SME Consultation inbound requests (90min) + 5 Eval Results Interpretation and Remediation Coaching sessions (45min) = +15 occurrences, +2.25hrs. Cumulative 50 across 14 cycles. PM explicitly referenced as 'Evals expert', 'person to ask', 'SME on evals' across field enablement threads (AmEx, Lumen, MGM, ESS). BSI EMERGENCY escalated — requestTypes=6 (scoping, coverage, metrics, rubric design, timing, eval-driven development). EvalCon April 2 deadline T-13 days = peak amplification. Force-multiplier: unblocking this unblocks 7 downstream patterns. Metrics span: groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy, multi-turn coherence.

patterns.label: Eval Tooling CLI and Implementation Troubleshooting
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 6 new Tooling Access and Reality-Check Clarification requests (freq=6, 30min) covering access/permissions, SharePoint, CLI, MCP, Copilot Studio, Foundry, roadmap vs workaround questions (+6 occurrences, +0.5hrs). Cumulative 21 across 14 cycles. Pattern stabilized at ~5-6/cycle. Deterministic Q&A with known answer set — spawn from eval-coaching-and-scoping. Auto nudged +1 to 70 as answer corpus is well-characterized. PM acts as 'truth-teller setting expectations' on what tools can/cannot do today. Strong T1 automation candidate.

patterns.label: Loop Meeting Notes Creation and Post-Meeting Action Capture
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 4 Recurring Loop Meeting Notes maintenance occurrences (25min each) + 1 Eval Weekly Progress Update in Loop (30min) = +5 occurrences, +0.92hrs. Cumulative 29 across 14 cycles. PM creates structured Loop notes with consistent schema: Agenda, Meeting Notes, Decisions/Open Questions, Follow-up Tasks. Confirmed across both sources for 14 consecutive cycles — fully INSTITUTIONAL. Chain head for meeting-notes ecosystem. SGI 85 HIGH — same structure duplicated across all teams. Camp AIR cascade this cycle amplified downstream note creation demand.

patterns.label: Parallel Eval Enablement Deck Creation (Team-Wide Duplication)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 7 PowerPoint Deck Iterative Refinement cycles confirmed (freq=7, 120min) spanning MPRs, PM RoBs, pitch decks, monthly reviews, Camp AIR prep materials (+7 occurrences, +2.0hrs). Cumulative 16 across 14 cycles. Multiple people still independently creating eval decks (TFM Agent Evals, Evaluation.pptx, CAPE_EVALs_Playbook_v5). SGI 92 CRITICAL — parallelCreators=3, versionCount=5+, explicitly acknowledged in meetings. Auto nudged +1 to 85 — Camp AIR generated additional variant materials + EvalCon deadline April 2 at peak amplification. Final urgency window active.

patterns.label: External Partner and Customer Eval Sessions
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 3 External Customer Eval Syncs (AmEx, ESS, LCS project closure, freq=3, 165min) + 2 Customer/External Eval Escalation Threads (freq=2, 30min) = +5 occurrences, +3.25hrs. Cumulative 42.25hrs total. EDII 5.0+ confirmed for 14 cycles — external intensity fully structural. Pre-session prep and post-session follow-up remain prime automation targets. Links to parallel-eval-deck-creation SGI gap and eval-coaching bottleneck. Pilot Project Closure (AI E2E Global Customer Service) signals engagement lifecycle pattern.

patterns.label: Weekly Status Update Reporting (Parallel Team Effort)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: Eval Weekly Progress Update in Loop (freq=1, 30min) + Weekly Status Update Multi-Format Duplication (doc, freq=1, 35min) + Agent Eval Progress Report Rolling Versioned Docs (v2/v3/v4, freq=1, 45min) = +3 occurrences, +1.83hrs. Cumulative 6 occurrences. Multiple parallel artifacts confirmed: done/next/blockers Teams table, narrative Teams chat updates, email executive updates. SGI 88 CRITICAL — everyone writing same information in different systems simultaneously. Template-fill from Loop notes is high automation ceiling. Pattern INSTITUTIONAL.

patterns.label: Security and Compliance Alert Review
patterns.llmRationale: Not directly visible in cycle 36 signals (1-cycle gap — not marking as declining until 3+ cycles per policy). Last confirmed cycle 35 with 7 Purview/Office365 alerts (File Blocked, Admin elevation, Loop workspace). Cumulative 21 across 14 cycles. Machine-generated, classifiable, rule-based. Highest automation ceiling. Single-recipient limits org-wide value — personal productivity win only. Pattern MATURE and structurally flat.

patterns.label: CAPE and Evals Leadership Strategy Thread Participation
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 3 Evals Strategy and Leadership Thread participations (email, freq=3, 40min) + 1 Customer Donated Evals Governance Thread (email, freq=1, 25min) + 1 Weekly Project Crucible V-Team Meeting (meeting, freq=1, 25min) = +5 occurrences, +1.5hrs. Cumulative 19 across 14 cycles. Cross-org audience (CAPE, Copilot, leadership, partners). CAPE + FY26 agent evaluation + Project Crucible + customer donated evals playbook threads all active. Val nudged to 92 — EvalCon + Camp AIR together raise leadership visibility to peak. Digest and brief extraction automatable at ~60% ceiling; judgment required on positioning.

patterns.label: Recurring Weekly Meeting Portfolio (Calendar Density)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: Project Crucible V-Team (Mon, 25min) + Weekly Team Meeting Manager Directs (Mon, 40min) + CAPE Architecture Weekly (Mon, 55min) + Copilot Studio Office Hours x2 (Mon+Tue, 85min) + US Pod Stand-Up (Tue, 25min) + Large Forum All-Hands x3 (270min) = +9 occurrences, +8.33hrs. Cumulative 33.08hrs total. MPBI 14+. Camp AIR 480min immersive sessions dominate this cycle as separate pattern. Calendar density remains INSTITUTIONAL — all meetings confirmed extended through August. Pre-meeting briefing and post-meeting routing are automation surfaces.

patterns.label: Cross-Org Navigation and VOC Routing Assistance
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 4 Customer VoC Synthesis and Exec-Ready Summary requests (teams, freq=4, 40min): MPR blurbs, one-liners for leadership reviews, duplicate ask dedup (+4 occurrences, +0.67hrs). Cumulative 16 across 14 cycles. PM as informal routing layer between FDEs, MCS Analytics, and PG Eval teams. Codifiable via org chart + DRI mapping + routing rules. Downstream of eval-coaching bottleneck — resolving BSI EMERGENCY would reduce VOC routing volume proportionally.

patterns.label: EvalCon Content Planning and Positioning Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 4 EvalCon Submission and Content Readiness Coordination sessions (teams, freq=4, 35min) covering framing, process learnings, submission logistics, dry-run discussions, leadership alignment (+4 occurrences, +0.58hrs). Cumulative 17 across 14 cycles. EvalCon deadline now CONFIRMED April 2 (T-13 days from 2026-03-20) — Deadline Demand Amplification AT PEAK. Brief and submission doc scaffolding automatable. FORECAST: will transition to declining post-April 2.

patterns.label: Technical Artifact Distribution via Teams (Repos, ZIPs, Specs)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 3 artifact shares via Teams from Skills Library and AI Workflow Standardization discussion (repos, markdown SOPs, taxonomy docs, quality gate specs) = +3 occurrences, +0.5hrs. Cumulative 27 across 14 cycles. PM uses Teams as informal distribution hub. Skills-as-markdown-SOPs adds a new artifact class to this pattern's scope. Pattern MATURE at ~3-8 shares/cycle pace.

patterns.label: Agent Architecture and MCP/Skills Scoping Discussions
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: URGENT Declarative Agent SharePoint Escalation (email, freq=1, 20min) — agent grounded across 170+ SharePoint sites, broad CC list, multiple senior stakeholders, correctness/safety/readiness concerns + CAPE Architecture Weekly Call (meeting, 55min, declarative vs custom agents, convergence, migration) + Skills Library standardization threads. +3 occurrences, +0.83hrs. Cumulative 21 across 14 cycles. Val nudged to 85 — URGENT architectural thread elevates strategic visibility. Architecture FAQ and decision-tree components automatable (T1/T2).

patterns.label: CAPE Camp AIR Immersive Training and Hackathon Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: CAPE Camp AIR Multi-Day Immersive Sessions confirmed with freq=2, 480min total across Monday and Tuesday (mindset, workflow, Copilot productivity, vibe coding, Dataverse integration, agent creation, EOD debriefs). Largest contiguous time block on calendar — 8 hours of immersion crowding out deep-work time. +2 occurrences, +8.0hrs. Cumulative 53hrs total. Immersion Cascade Source confirmed: triggers downstream amplification across meeting-notes, status, artifacts, context patterns. Cascade dampening 0.7x applies. Quarterly cadence confirmed.

patterns.label: Skill-Detector Technical Spec Iterative Build Cycle
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 5 Skills Library and AI Workflow Standardization Discussion occurrences (teams, freq=5, 50min) — skills-as-markdown-SOPs, taxonomy, quality gates, contribution cadence, reuse criteria + 2 PRD and Spec Format Fragmentation occurrences (document, freq=2, 30min) — SpecKit, one-page brief to full spec workflow fragmentation = +7 occurrences, +1.33hrs. Cumulative 23 across 14 cycles. Meta-pattern: the Skilluminator workflow itself is a detectable repeating pattern (Self-Referential archetype). PRD/spec fragmentation adds new sub-class.

patterns.label: Agent Eval Sets Template Maintenance (Recurring Excel Tracker)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: CAPE Eval Engagement Tracker Maintenance (document, freq=3, 20min) — customers, engagement type, record dates, living document updated by PM + Customer Success Lead + Agent Eval Progress Report Rolling Versioned Docs (freq=1, 45min, v2/v3/v4 all March 2026) = +4 occurrences, +1.08hrs. Cumulative 7 across 14 cycles. Agent Eval Sets_Template.xlsx still in active use. Feeds external-partner-eval-sessions pattern. Versioned rolling reports add a new lifecycle artifact class.

patterns.label: Daily Personal Day-Prep Planning Page
patterns.llmRationale: Not directly visible in cycle 36 signals (1-cycle gap — not marking as declining until 3+ cycles per policy). Last confirmed cycle 35 with 5 day-prep files ([Mar 19] 'Help me prepare for my day' pattern). Recurring morning planning artifact. Single-participant limits org value, but high personal productivity. Calendar digest + email triage + priority summary automation. Connects to meeting-load-triage and ado-notification-triage as upstream data sources.

patterns.label: High-Urgency Escalation Email Authoring
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 2 Customer and External Eval Escalation Threads (email, freq=2, 30min) — external customer, support engineer, field partner, higher per-thread attention, PM as SME/escalation point (+2 occurrences, +0.5hrs). Also URGENT Declarative Agent SharePoint Escalation partially feeds this (counted in agent-architecture-scoping). Cumulative 7 across 14 cycles. PM escalation tone is distinctive and consistent. Draft generation from structured context is T2 automatable. Low-frequency but consistent pattern.

patterns.label: Viva Engage and Community Digest Consumption
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 5 Org Broadcast and Internal Announcement Scanning emails (email, freq=5, 15min) — EvalCon, BIC All Hands, BAT All Hands, Microsoft Daily Digest, Viva Engage. Mostly marked as read, light scanning (+5 occurrences, +0.25hrs). Cumulative 20. Passive consumption, single-participant. Low strategic signal density. Anti-pattern: Community Noise. Continuing decline trajectory — low value ceiling and single-participant scope. Summarization + topic flagging possible but org-wide value minimal.

patterns.label: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invite Dispatch
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 2 Workspace Collaboration Invitation Emails (email, freq=2, 5min) — 'You have been looped in! Skilluminator' subject pattern, action-oriented messages looping collaborators into workspaces (+2 occurrences, +0.08hrs). Cumulative 6 across 3 tracked cycles. Auto-generated by M365 when collaboration initiated. Low time impact but indicates collaboration setup pattern. Consistent 2/cycle volume. Monitor for growth.

patterns.label: Skilluminator Cycle Dashboard Weekly HTML Refresh
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: Eval Enablement Dashboard Ongoing Maintenance confirmed (document, freq=1, 20min) — resource hub created and referenced in multiple weekly/standing meetings, maintained dashboard updated over time (+1 occurrence, +0.33hrs). Cumulative 3 across 3 tracked cycles. Meta-pattern: the Skilluminator workflow (signal ingestion → pattern detection → dashboard refresh → refinement loop) is itself a detectable repeating pattern (Self-Referential Meta-Pattern archetype). Auto nudged to 87 — cycle refresh cadence is highly automatable.

patterns.label: 1:1 Stakeholder Check-Ins (Weekly Portfolio)
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 36: 2 Recurring 1:1 Manager Check-Ins confirmed (meeting, freq=2, 50min) — Mondays and Thursdays 2:35-3:00 PM, highly stable recurring 1:1 with iterative shaping of direction, eval-driven development framing (+2 occurrences, +0.83hrs). Cumulative 14 across 3 tracked cycles. Relationship-heavy (low automation ceiling for the meeting itself) but pre-meeting prep and talking point generation are automatable at T1. Feeds meeting-load-triage portfolio.

patterns.label: Discussion to Meeting to Document Sequential Workflow Chain
patterns.llmRationale: NEW PATTERN — first confirmed cycle 36. Two cross-channel workflow chains detected: (1) Discussion-to-Meeting-to-Doc: conversation starts in Teams/email → meeting scheduled → Loop document created/updated with decisions and follow-ups (freq=3, 60min); (2) DevOps-Alert-to-Sync-to-Doc: system-generated email (ADO/TAP/eval results) → sync or review meeting → artifact creation or Loop tracking (freq=4, 50min). Combined 7 occurrences across 110min. Represents automated pipeline opportunity: trigger detection → meeting prep scaffolding → post-meeting doc generation. Multi-source nature elevates generalizability. Watch for growth.

candidateSkills.name: eval-design-advisor
candidateSkills.description: Answers recurring eval strategy and scoping questions posed to the PM expert bottleneck. Covers: how many eval cases are enough, which metrics to use (groundedness, Compare Meaning, tool_call_accuracy, multi-turn coherence), when in the agent lifecycle evals should run, rubric design, and eval-driven development. Routes complex cases to PM (T3) while fully handling FAQ and framework questions (T1). Includes eval-tooling CLI troubleshooting as a sub-module.
triggerExamples: How many eval cases do I need for my Copilot agent?
What metrics should I use to measure groundedness?
When in the agent lifecycle should I start running evals?
Can you walk me through eval-driven development?
My CSV import is failing in the eval CLI — what's wrong?
Is tool_call_accuracy available in preview or GA?
What do these eval results actually mean? How do we act on failures?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates the #1 expert bottleneck (BSI EMERGENCY). PM handles 50 cumulative eval guidance requests across 14 cycles — 15 new this cycle alone (10 SME consultations + 5 results interpretation coaching sessions). Deflecting T1 requests frees ~2 hrs/week and unblocks the 7-pattern Eval Ecosystem. EvalCon April 2 deadline T-13 days = urgency at absolute peak.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Generates structured Loop meeting notes from meeting transcript or spoken summary. Output schema: Agenda, Meeting Notes, Decisions/Open Questions, Follow-up Tasks with owners and dates. Integrates with Teams and Loop. Chain head for the largest pattern ecosystem (downstream: weekly-status, action-owner-chasing, transcript-to-loop).
triggerExamples: Create Loop notes for the meeting I just had
Extract action items from this meeting transcript
Generate a structured follow-up doc from these notes
Capture decisions and owners from today's sync
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Chain head for meeting-notes ecosystem. SGI 85 HIGH — every team duplicates this structure. 29 confirmed occurrences across 14 cycles. INSTITUTIONAL pattern. Camp AIR 480min immersion cascade this cycle amplified downstream demand. Standardizing saves estimated 4-6 hrs/week across participants.

candidateSkills.name: ado-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Classifies and triages Azure DevOps work-item email notifications by type (Task, Requirement, Bug, Feature, Epic, IcM) and routes them to appropriate attention queues. Suppresses subscriber-only noise, surfaces actionable assignee items, and links to relevant meeting or document context. Also handles DevOps-Alert-to-Sync-to-Doc workflow triggers.
triggerExamples: Which ADO notifications need my action today?
Filter my work-item emails — show only what I'm assigned to
Triage this week's ADO notification digest
Route this Bug notification to the right team
candidateSkills.valueProposition: 94 cumulative ADO notifications + workflow triggers across 14 cycles at steady 30+/week — fully INSTITUTIONAL. PM is subscriber (not assignee) in most. Automation ceiling 93%. Estimated 2-3 hrs/week reclaimed at scale. Now also covers Alert-to-Sync-to-Doc pipeline variant (cycle 36 new sub-class).

candidateSkills.name: eval-enablement-deck-standardizer
candidateSkills.description: Generates or updates a standard eval enablement deck from a canonical template, tailored for specific customer segment, agent type, or partner tier. Eliminates parallel deck creation problem (SGI 92 CRITICAL) by producing one parameterized output from a single source of truth.
triggerExamples: Create an eval intro deck for this customer
Update the CAPE evals playbook for this partner session
Generate a pitch deck for eval-driven development for an SI partner
Build an EvalCon session deck from our process learnings
candidateSkills.valueProposition: SGI 92 CRITICAL for 14 consecutive cycles. 16 cumulative occurrences, 3 parallel creators, 6.5 hrs in redundant creation. Camp AIR + EvalCon April 2 deadline together create peak amplification window — this is the final urgency cycle before post-EvalCon transition.

candidateSkills.name: cross-channel-workflow-orchestrator
candidateSkills.description: Detects the Discussion-to-Meeting-to-Document workflow chain and provides automated scaffolding at each transition point: pre-meeting brief from chat context, post-meeting Loop doc generation from meeting notes, action item extraction with owner assignment. Covers both organic discussion chains and DevOps-Alert-to-Sync-to-Doc pipeline variants.
triggerExamples: We just decided to schedule a meeting about X — create a pre-meeting brief
I got an ADO alert on eval pipeline failures — prep a review doc
This Teams thread turned into a meeting — create the Loop notes template
Extract follow-up tasks from this discussion-to-meeting chain
candidateSkills.valueProposition: NEW in cycle 36 — first detection of cross-channel workflow orchestration gap. 7 occurrences across 110min. Represents a force-multiplier opportunity: automating the handoff points between email/Teams → meeting → document reduces context loss and saves transition overhead across all 3 channels simultaneously.
